name: AI
text: AI: Artificial Intelligence refers to the ability of machines to perform tasks that would normally require human intelligence. e.g. Chatbots or Voice Assistant.

showLogic.text: Show if respondent selected code [AI] at S8.DT.AI: Artificial Intelligence refers to the ability of machines to perform tasks that would normally require human intelligence. e.g. Chatbots or Voice Assistant.

name: DT
text: AI: Artificial Intelligence refers to the ability of machines to perform tasks that would normally require human intelligence. e.g. Chatbots or Voice Assistant.

name: XR
text: XR: Extended Reality technologies use computer-generated images or overlay digital information onto the real world, creating immersive and interactive experiences for the user    •     AR: AR is like putting a digital sticker on the real world through devices like Phone or Tablet.    •     VR: VR involves the use of headsets to immerse the user in a fully digital environment.    •     MR: MR is like placing a virtual object in the real world that looks like it is really there and can interact with the environment.

showLogic.text: Show if respondent selected code [ExtendedReality] at S8.DT.XR: Extended Reality technologies use computer-generated images or overlay digital information onto the real world, creating immersive and interactive experiences for the user    •     AR: AR is like putting a digital sticker on the real world through devices like Phone or Tablet.    •     VR: VR involves the use of headsets to immerse the user in a fully digital environment.    •     MR: MR is like placing a virtual object in the real world that looks like it is really there and can interact with the environment.

name: DT
text: XR: Extended Reality technologies use computer-generated images or overlay digital information onto the real world, creating immersive and interactive experiences for the user    •     AR: AR is like putting a digital sticker on the real world through devices like Phone or Tablet.    •     VR: VR involves the use of headsets to immerse the user in a fully digital environment.    •     MR: MR is like placing a virtual object in the real world that looks like it is really there and can interact with the environment.
